```{r, echo=FALSE, results="hide"}
p <- sample(seq(0.10,0.90,0.05),1)
```

Question
========
Se $X\sim\operatorname{Bernoulli}(`r p`)$, qual é sua fgm?

Answerlist
----------
* $M_X(t)=1-`r p`+`r p`e^t$.
* $M_X(t)=`r p`+(1-`r p`)e^t$.
* $M_X(t)=e^{`r p`t}$.
* $M_X(t)=`r p`e^t$.
* $M_X(t)=(1-`r p`+`r p`t)$.

Solution
========
Como $X$ vale 0 com probabilidade $1-p$ e 1 com probabilidade $p$,
$$M_X(t)=E(e^{tX})=(1-p)e^0+pe^t=1-p+pe^t.$$

Meta-information
================
exname: Cap1S9Q6_fgm_Bernoulli
extype: schoice
exsolution: 10000
exshuffle: TRUE
